chief_complaint: A 16-year-old immunocompetent female from Vietnam presents with acute meningoencephalitis of unknown origin.
history: Hepatitis B virus (HBV) infection remains a major global health burden, particularly in low- and middle-income countries (LMICs). Although primarily hepatotropic, HBV has occasionally been linked to extrahepatic and neurological manifestations. The patient presented with acute meningoencephalitis of unknown origin.
exam: No specific physical examination findings are mentioned.
labs: Conventional microbiological and molecular tests were negative. Retrospective next-generation sequencing of cerebrospinal fluid revealed HBV genotype B2, with a viral load of 3.2 x 10^4 copies/mL confirmed by quantitative PCR and a positive hepatitis B surface antigen result.
